title: Staff Full Stack Developer - React / Node
description:
**Who are we?**
 Equinix is the world’s digital infrastructure company®, shortening the path to connectivity to enable the innovations that enrich our work, life and planet.
   

  

 A place where bold ideas are welcomed, human connection is valued, and everyone has the opportunity to shape their future.
   

  

 Help us challenge assumptions, uncover bias, and remove barriers—because progress starts with fresh ideas. You’ll find belonging, purpose, and a team that welcomes you—because when you feel valued, you’re empowered to do your best work.
   

  

**About The Role**
 Equinix is seeking a highly motivated Staff Software Engineer with a strong focus on Digital Experience to join our Bangalore office. In this role, you will design, build, and launch scalable software solutions that meet customer requirements using a state\\-of\\-the\\-art technology stack and best engineering practices. You will be responsible for building critical applications and features at a global scale, delivering a world\\-class experience for the Equinix procurement function.
   

  

 As a Staff Software Engineer, you will own the technical roadmap, serve as the primary technical SME, and collaborate across IT, security, and business teams to deliver secure, compliant, and scalable AI\\-driven applications.
   

  

 This role is ideal for a highly skilled engineer passionate about leveraging cutting\\-edge technologies across the full stack—modern front\\-end frameworks such as React.js, Angular, Vue.js, and HTML5, combined with robust back\\-end platforms like Node.js, Express.js, Django, and Spring Boot—to solve complex, real\\-world problems. We’re looking for someone with exceptional engineering fundamentals, a deep understanding of scalable architecture, and strong product intuition to deliver high\\-impact solutions with precision.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

* Front\\-End Development
* Design and deliver responsive, scalable, and accessible (WCAG 2\\.2 AA) world\\-class UI/UX solutions using React and TypeScript
* Ensure strong state management, performance optimization, and intuitive user experiences for large\\-scale audiences.
* Understand the bigger picture and all user personas to design and build secure, scalable applications that deliver a seamless experience and create a true WOW factor for customers.
* Back\\-End \\& Microservices
* Build and maintain secure, scalable microservices using Java (Spring Boot/Quarkus/Micronaut) and Node.js (NestJS/Express).
* Define API contracts (REST/GraphQL/gRPC), govern schemas (Avro/Protobuf), and ensure consistency, idempotency, and observability.
* Architecture \\& System Design
* Develop end\\-to\\-end product flows and robust system architectures.
* Architect secure, multi\\-cloud applications across AWS/GCP/Azure; design portable, resilient solutions; manage containerization and Kubernetes deployments (Helm/Kustomize, GitOps).
* Apply domain\\-driven design (DDD) principles for modeling and modular architecture.
* DevOps \\& CI/CD
* Establish CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/Azure DevOps.
* Implement progressive delivery strategies (canary/blue\\-green) and feature flagging (LaunchDarkly).
* Security \\& Compliance
* Enforce security by design: OAuth2/OIDC/SAML, JWT, RBAC; manage secrets (Vault); ensure supply\\-chain security (image scanning).
* Observability \\& Operational Excellence
* Instrument services with OpenTelemetry; implement logging, metrics, and tracing (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK, Jaeger).
* Define SLOs/SLIs and create incident response/runbooks.
* Collaboration \\& Leadership
* Work closely with Product Managers, SRE/Platform, and SecOps teams to deliver integrated, compliant solutions.
* Mentor junior engineers, conduct code reviews, and foster an inclusive, collaborative environment.
* Innovation \\& Continuous Improvement
* Evaluate emerging technologies (especially AI/ML), run proof\\-of\\-concepts, and recommend adoption strategies with clear ROI and risk trade\\-offs.


**Required Qualifications**
* 4 \\- 8 years of experience designing, building, and scaling enterprise\\-grade applications that deliver measurable business impact and exceptional user experiences.
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent experience.
* Proven track record in developing end\\-to\\-end customer\\-focused solutions, including buying/procurement experiences or similar applications.

**To Be Successful, You Will**

* Good Hands on experience with Core Java, Maven / Gradle, REST API, REST ASSURED/Karate, Selenium(WebDriver), Appium, Junit, TestNG, Java IDE’s.
* Good understanding of HTTP, JSON protocol and syntax, Cucumber, BDD methodology, Gherkin syntax, JIRA, Jenkins (CICD exposure) and Confluence.
* Working experience in Agile methodology / SAFe with ‘in\\-sprint’ automation

 You’ll achieve more when you join HSBC.

title: Smalltalk
description:
Role Description
   

  

 The Smalltalk Developer plays a critical role in maintaining and evolving legacy and mission critical enterprise systems that power core business operations in the bank. These systems often built decades ago continue to serve as the backbone for high volume transaction processing and require continuous enhancement modernization and integration with newer digital platforms. This role is essential in ensuring business continuity operational stability and incremental modernization of these systems while aligning them with current business demands. The developer works closely with cross functional teams to bridge the gap between legacy architecture and modern enterprise ecosystems enabling organizations to innovate without disrupting core services.
   

  

 Job Responsibilities
   

  

 Analyze and understand existing Smalltalk based applications that support critical business processes
   

  

 Enhance and maintain legacy systems to support new business requirements regulatory changes and performance improvements
   

  

 Collaborate with business stakeholders to interpret requirements and translate them into system changes
   

  

 Perform impact analysis to ensure changes do not disrupt interconnected systems or downstream processes
   

  

 Troubleshoot production issues identify root causes and implement fixes to ensure system stability
   

  

 Support integration of legacy Smalltalk systems with modern platforms APIs and external services
   

  

 Participate in system testing validation and release activities to ensure smooth deployment
   

  

 Work closely with QA and operations teams to ensure high availability and reliability of applications
   

  

 Contribute to documentation of system behavior workflows and enhancements for long term maintainability
   

  

 Assist in modernization initiatives such as refactoring migration planning or hybrid architecture implementation
   

  

 Technical Skills
   

  

 Strong proficiency in Smalltalk programming VisualWorks GemStone S or Squeak
   

  

 Experience working with legacy enterprise systems and codebases
   

  

 Solid understanding of object oriented programming concepts and design patterns
   

  

 Experience in debugging troubleshooting and performance tuning of applications
   

  

 Familiarity with integrating legacy systems with modern technologies including APIs databases and middleware

title: Sr. Software Engineer (Contract)
description:
Sanas.ai is pioneering the future of human communication. Founded by a team of Stanford researchers and entrepreneurs with deep industry experience, Sanas has developed the world’s first real\\-time speech transformation platform capable of accent translation, noise elimination, speech enhancement, and cross\\-language communication.
   

  

 Sanas makes conversations clearer, more inclusive, and more effective, removing barriers that prevent people from being understood, regardless of accent, background noise, or native language.
   

  

 Since going to market in 2023, Sanas has scaled at an extraordinary pace, growing from $0 to $32M ARR in under two years, with a projected \\>$50M ARR by the end of 2025\\. The company recently recorded its first $10M quarter and is on track to achieve $120M in ARR next year.
   

  

 The company’s valuation has a clear trajectory toward multi\\-billion\\-dollar market capitalization as it continues to expand into new verticals and product categories. With a TAM that spans all human in the loop communications and beyond, Sanas has the potential to impact every industry and every global interaction.
   

  

 Sanas is revolutionizing the way we communicate with the world’s first real\\-time algorithm, designed to modulate accents, eliminate background noises, and magnify speech clarity. Pioneered by seasoned startup founders with a proven track record of creating and steering multiple unicorn companies, our groundbreaking GDP\\-shifting technology sets a gold standard.
   

  

 Sanas is a 200\\-strong team, established in 2020\\. In this short span, we’ve successfully secured over $100 million in funding. Our innovation has been supported by the industry’s leading investors, including Insight Partners, Google Ventures, Quadrille Capital, General Catalyst, Quiet Capital, and other influential investors. Our reputation is further solidified by collaborations with numerous Fortune 100 companies. With Sanas, you’re not just adopting a product; you’re investing in the future of communication.
   

  

**Job Description**
 We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Senior / Software Engineer to play a pivotal role in the development and evolution of our flagship desktop application, which is a cross\\-platform App. You will leverage your deep expertise in C\\+\\+ / Flutter / JS, and RESTful API integration to contribute to the design, implementation, and optimization of key features across our SDK and frontend. This role offers an exciting opportunity to tackle complex technical challenges and make significant contributions to a high\\-impact product.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Contribute to the architectural design and implementation of the desktop application, focusing on the C\\+\\+ App\\-core/SDK, Flutter frontend, and their interaction with backend systems via REST APIs.
* Develop and enhance the app business logic, user interface, ensuring a responsive and intuitive user experience.
* Implement and integrate with RESTful APIs to communicate with backend services (Authentication, PostgreSQL, Clickhouse, etc.).
* Ensure the performance, reliability, and scalability of the application components you contribute to.
* Write comprehensive unit and integration tests to ensure code quality.
* Participate actively in code reviews, providing and receiving constructive feedback.
* Troubleshoot and debug complex issues spanning the frontend, SDK, and backend interactions.
* Collaborate closely with other engineers, architects, and product managers to deliver high\\-quality software.
* Contribute to technical documentation and knowledge sharing within the team.
* Stay up\\-to\\-date with the latest advancements in Flutter, and related technologies.


**Must Have Qualifications**
* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or a related field.
* 4\\+ years in software development.
* Deep expertise in at least one of the core areas: Flutter frontend development, or RESTful API integration.
* Strong understanding of software development principles, design patterns, and best
* Experience of developing and working with CI/CD pipeline, focusing on build automation, unit testing, static and dynamic code analysis.
* Experience with cross\\-platform desktop application development is a plus.
* Familiarity with database systems such as PostgreSQL and Clickhouse.
* Proficiency with version control systems (e.g., Git).
* Excellent problem\\-solving, analytical, and communication skills.
* Ability to work effectively both independently and as part 1 of a collaborative team.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience with performance optimization techniques for desktop applications.
* Contributions to open\\-source projects in C\\+\\+ or Flutter.
* Experience with other frontend or backend technologies.
* Experience in an agile development environment.


 Joining us means contributing to the world’s first real\\-time speech understanding platform revolutionizing Contact Centers and Enterprises alike.
   

  

 Our technology empowers agents, transforms customer experiences, and drives measurable growth. But this is just the beginning. You'll be part of a team exploring the vast potential of an increasingly sonic future.

 We are seeking a skilled Senior Snowflake Developer with strong expertise in Data Vault modeling and cloud\\-based data engineering. The ideal candidate will design, develop, and optimize scalable data solutions using Snowflake and modern ETL/ELT tools in a cloud environment.
   

  

 Your future duties and responsibilities
   

  

 Design, develop, and maintain data pipelines using Snowflake and DBT.
   

  

 Implement Data Vault modeling, schema design, and performance tuning.
   

  

 Build and optimize ETL/ELT workflows for structured and semi\\-structured data.
   

  

 Automate workflows using Snowflake features such as Tasks, Streams, and Stored Procedures.
   

  

 Ensure data quality, governance, and security compliance.
   

  

 Troubleshoot and optimize existing data workflows for performance and reliability.
   

  

 Support migration of legacy data warehouses to Snowflake.
   

  

 Collaborate with cross\\-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver solutions.
   

  

 Stay updated with the latest Snowflake features and industry best practices.
   

  

 Must\\-Have Skills
   

  

 Strong hands\\-on experience with Snowflake Cloud Data Platform.
   

  

 Experience in Data Vault modeling.
   

  

 Strong proficiency in SQL and data warehousing concepts.
   

  

 Experience with ETL/ELT tools such as DBT, Informatica, Talend, Matillion, or Fivetran.
   

  

 Experience working with cloud platforms (AWS / Azure / GCP).
   

  

 Knowledge of Snowflake performance tuning and optimization techniques.
   

  

 Experience in automating workflows using Snowflake Tasks, Streams, and Procedures.
   

  

 Familiarity with Python, Scala, or Java for scripting and automation.
   

  

 Strong problem\\-solving skills and attention to detail.
   

  

 Good\\-to\\-Have Skills
   

  

 Exposure to big data technologies such as Apache Spark or Hadoop.
   

  

 Experience with BI tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Looker.
   

  

 Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices.
   

  

 Prior experience in data migration projects to Snowflake.
   

  

 Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments.
   

  

**Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role**
 Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field.
   

  

**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**
 Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…
   

  

 You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.
   

  

 Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.
   

  

 You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well\\-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.
   

  

 Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

title: Observability Engineering Lead
description:
**What if you could build** 
 a career where ambition meets innovation?
   

  

 At LPL’s Global Capability Center, you'll find a collaborative culture where your voice matters, integrity guides every decision, and technology fuels progress. Your skills, talents, and ideas will redefine what's possible. LPL's success reflects its exceptional employees, who together pursue one noble purpose: empowering financial advisors to deliver personalized advice for all who need it. We’re proud to be expanding and reaching new heights in Hyderabad.
   

  

 Join us as we create something extraordinary together.
   

  

**Job Overview**
 We are seeking a dynamic, motivated and experienced head of Observability Engineering within LPL SRE organization. In this role you will drive LPL’s enterprise observability posture, standards, ensuring end‑to‑end visibility, actionable insights, and proactive detection across applications, platforms, and infrastructure. This role involves working with the observability architect to establish the architectural vision, standards, and reference patterns for observability \\- spanning metrics, traces, logs, RUM/UI visibility, routing, enrichment, and retention.
   

  

 This is an exciting opportunity to drive meaningful change and enhancing the advisor and investor experience. If you are passionate about production operations, stability, SRE and Observability and have a track record of success, we invite you to apply and be part of our journey toward greater resilience and efficiency.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Develop and maintain a multi‑year observability architecture roadmap aligned with enterprise modernization initiatives.
* Define the target state for observability platforms, data pipelines, and telemetry strategy.
* Establish a unified architectural vision for cross\\-domain observability, automation, and intelligent insights (AIOps).
* Observability Platform License and Cost Optimization
* Work with the observability architect to define and build telemetry quality frameworks, log pipelines, cardinality, data governance, PII handling, and access controls.
* Establish and build standards for SLO/SLI definitions, alerting best practices, and error\\-budget\\-driven improvement.
* Review major incidents, identify observability gaps, and evolve architecture and tooling.
* Design and build end‑to‑end observability patterns across UI, RUM, metrics, traces, logs, enrichment, routing, and retention.
* Provide guidance and build reusable assets (templates, SDKs, reliability/observability\\-as\\-code patterns).
* Enable frictionless onboarding and self\\-service integrations into telemetry pipelines and dashboards.
* Build, lead and empower a high performing team of observability engineers


 What are we looking for?
   

  

 We’re looking for strong collaborators who deliver exceptional client experiences and thrive in fast\\-paced, team\\-oriented environments. Our ideal candidates
 **pursue greatness** 
 ,
 **act with integrity** 
 , and are
 **driven to help our clients succeed** 
 . We value those who embrace creativity, continuous improvement, and contribute to a culture where we
 **win together** 
 and
 **create and share joy** 
 in our work.
   

  

**Requirements**
* Strong knowledge of observability tooling and ecosystem (Open Telemetry, Dynatrace, ELK, Datadog, Grafana, Splunk, etc.).
* Proficiency in designing logging, metrics, tracing, and alerting pipelines.
* Familiarity with SRE principles, reliability patterns, AIOps, and event correlation strategies.
* Experience defining enterprise\\-level telemetry and resilience standards.
* Exceptional cross\\-team collaboration, communication, and architectural storytelling.


**Core Competencies**
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a focus on collaboration and relationship\\-building.
* Able to communicate effectively with CXOs
* Ability to influence and drive change across the organization.
* Analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable insights.
* Achieve a 30% reduction in MTTD and MTTR within the first year of operation
* Able to identify offending service and root cause for at least 70% of incidents within 15\\-20 mins using observability platforms
* Foster a culture of continuous improvement and knowledge\\-sharing initiatives that empower team members.

title: Senior SAS Administration
description:
Position Description
   

  

 At CGI, we’re a team of builders. We call our employees members because all who join CGI are building their own company \\- one that has grown to 72,000 professionals located in 40 countries. Founded in 1976, CGI is a leading IT and business process services firm committed to helping clients succeed. We have the global resources, expertise, stability and dedicated professionals needed to achieve. At CGI, we’re a team of builders. We call our employees members because all who join CGI are building their own company \\- one that has grown to 72,000 professionals located in 40 countries. Founded in 1976, CGI is a leading IT and business process services firm committed to helping clients succeed. We have the global resources, expertise, stability and dedicated professionals needed to achieve results for our clients \\- and for our members. Come grow with us. Learn more at www.cgi.com.
   

  

 This is a great opportunity to join a winning team. CGI offers a competitive compensation package with opportunities for growth and professional development. Benefits for full\\-time, permanent members start on the first day of employment and include a paid time\\-off program and profit participation and stock purchase plans. We wish to thank all applicants for their interest and effort in applying for this position, however, only candidates selected for interviews will be contacted. No unsolicited agency referrals please.
   

  

 Job Title: SAS Admin
   

  

 Position: Software Engineer / Senior Software Engineer
   

  

 Experience: 4 \\- 6 Years
   

  

 Category: Software Development/ Engineering
   

  

 Main location: India, Bangalore
   

  

 Position ID: J1225\\-2215
   

  

 Employment Type: Full Time
   

  

 Your future duties and responsibilities
   

  

 SAS Viya 3\\.5 Administration Skill Set
   

  

 This requires a more modern "DevOps" mindset. You'll spend less time in GUIs and more time on the command line and with automation tools.
   

  

 Key Tools \\& Concepts:
   

  

* Ansible: This is non\\-negotiable for Viya 3\\.5\\. Deployment: You must understand how to run Ansible playbooks to deploy, update, or uninstall the SAS Viya environment. Configuration: Modifying vars.yml files to customize the deployment (e.g., adding TLS, changing ports, configuring authentication).
* Linux Expertise: Viya 3\\.5 runs exclusively on Linux. Service Management: Using systemd to manage all the microservices that comprise the Viya platform. Log Analysis: Using journalctl to query the centralized logs from all microservices. System Tuning: Understanding and modifying kernel parameters (sysctl), user limits (ulimits), and managing yum or apt package repositories.
* CAS (Cloud Analytic Services) Administration: Understanding the distributed, in\\-memory architecture of the CAS server. Managing Caslibs (the Viya equivalent of librefs), including permissions and data loading. Managing CAS server settings, nodes, and troubleshooting performance.
* Command\\-Line Interface (CLI): The sas\\-admin CLI is a primary day\\-to\\-day tool. Managing users and groups, content (reports, folders), and CAS settings.
* SAS Environment Manager (Viya Edition): The web\\-based UI for Viya. While the CLI is powerful, the UI is used for monitoring CAS, managing user/group rules, promoting content, and viewing service status.


 General Skills
   

  

 These are the foundational skills that bridge both platforms.
   

  

* Authentication \\& Security: Deep understanding of how SAS integrates with enterprise identity providers like LDAP or Active Directory. Experience configuring single sign\\-on (SSO) is a major plus.
* Networking: Solid knowledge of ports, firewalls, DNS, load balancers, and HTTPS/TLS. You will constantly be troubleshooting connectivity issues.
* Storage: Understanding of shared file systems (like NFS) is critical, as both platforms rely on it for configuration, user homes, and data access.
* Problem\\-Solving: A systematic, analytical approach to troubleshooting is the most important skill. You need to be able to isolate a problem (Is it the user? The client? The network? The mid\\-tier? The back\\-end?) by effectively using logs and monitoring tools.
* Communication: The ability to clearly communicate with end\\-users (analysts, data scientists), developers, and other IT infrastructure teams.


 For a job application, you should clearly separate your experience on your resume, perhaps with a "SAS 9\\.4 Administration" section and a "SAS Viya 3\\.5 Administration" section, highlighting the specific tools and accomplishments for each.
   

  

**Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role**
 skill : SAS admin, SAS Viya 3\\.5
   

  

 Exp : 4 to 6 yrs
   

  

**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**
 Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…
   

  

 You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.
   

  

 Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.
   

  

 You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well\\-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.
   

  

 Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.
